title: Strange Case of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde
author: Robert Louis Stevenson

setting.note: The respectable Dr Jekyll brews a potion that frees his hidden appetites as the monstrous Mr Hyde, and the lawyer Utterson traces the growing horror through a fog-bound London toward a locked cabinet and a confession. Stevenson's city is a real, gaslit London of double lives: the fine house with its sinister back door, the squalid Soho lodgings of Hyde, and the lanes where his violence erupts, all mapping the divided self onto the divided town.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Robert Louis Stevenson (d. 1894), first published 1886. Mapped as a cited work: we store no text and reproduce no passages here. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. This entry reproduces no text; the public-domain original is widely available.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. The original text is in the public domain.
rights.attribution: Strange Case of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde, Robert Louis Stevenson (1886). Locations mapped by Read the Places.

palette.note: The fog-grey of a gaslit London, the murk in which the respectable doctor and his monstrous other move as one, the divided self of the divided city. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
